steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: An illustration of “determination to experience the countryside” requires concrete proof: specific natural imagery showing where the speaker will go. The strongest signal is the presence of vivid countryside details—actual places and natural elements that demonstrate the experience.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: C is the only quotation where the speaker declares their will (“I'll walk where my own nature would be leading”; “It vexes me to choose another guide”) and immediately illustrates this with concrete countryside details—grey flocks in ferny glens and wind on the mountainside. A mentions returning to feelings without countryside details, B only lists rejected paths, and D focuses on rejecting imagination rather than embracing nature. That matches choice C — C is correct.
